NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.NRSV: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, for the sake of the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus,
text.Rotherham: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus, through the will of God,—according to the promise of life in Christ Jesus;—

text.NRSV: To Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
text.Rotherham: Unto Timothy, my beloved child: Favour, mercy, peace, from God our Father, and Christ Jesus our Lord.

text.NRSV: I am grateful to God—whom I worship with a clear conscience, as my ancestors did—when I remember you constantly in my prayers night and day.
text.Rotherham: Grateful, am I, unto God,—unto whom I am rendering divine service from my progenitors in a pure conscience, that, incessant, hold I the remembrance, concerning thee, in my supplications; day and night,

text.NRSV: Recalling your tears, I long to see you so that I may be filled with joy.
text.Rotherham: longing to see thee, being mindful of thy tears,—in order that, with joy, I may be filled:

text.NRSV: I am reminded of your sincere faith, a faith that lived first in your grandmother Lois and your mother Eunice and now, I am sure, lives in you.
text.Rotherham: A reminder, having received, of the unfeigned faith, that is in thee,—such, as dwelt first in thy grandmother Lois, and in thy mother Eunice,—I am persuaded, moreover, that [it dwelleth], in thee also.

text.NRSV: For this reason I remind you to rekindle the gift of God that is within you through the laying on of my hands;
text.Rotherham: For which cause, I put thee in remembrance, to be stirring up God’s gift of favour, which is in thee through means of the laying on of my hands;

text.NRSV: for God did not give us a spirit of cowardice, but rather a spirit of power and of love and of self-discipline.
text.Rotherham: For God hath not given us a spirit of cowardice, but of power, and love, and correction.

text.NRSV: Do not be ashamed, then, of the testimony about our Lord or of me his prisoner, but join with me in suffering for the gospel, relying on the power of God,
text.Rotherham: Be not thou, therefore, put to shame—with regard to the witness of our Lord, nor with regard to me his prisoner; but suffer hardship together with the glad-message according to the power of God,—

text.NRSV: who saved us and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works but according to his own purpose and grace. This grace was given to us in Christ Jesus before the ages began,
text.Rotherham: Who hath saved us, and called us with a holy calling, not according to our works, but according to the peculiar purpose and favour—which was given to us in Christ Jesus before age-during times,

text.NRSV: but it has now been revealed through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus, who abolished death and brought life and immortality to light through the gospel.
text.Rotherham: But hath now been made manifest through means of the forthshining of our Saviour Christ Jesus,—Who, indeed, hath abolished death, and hath thrown light upon life and incorruptibility, through means of the glad-message:

text.NRSV: For this gospel I was appointed a herald and an apostle and a teacher,
text.Rotherham: Whereunto, I, have been appointed herald and apostle and teacher;

text.NRSV: and for this reason I suffer as I do. But I am not ashamed, for I know the one in whom I have put my trust, and I am sure that he is able to guard until that day what I have entrusted to him.
text.Rotherham: For which cause, these things also, am I suffering; nevertheless I am not being put to shame, for I know him whom I have believed, and am persuaded that he is, able, to guard, that which I have entrusted, [to him] unto, that, day.

text.NRSV: Hold to the standard of sound teaching that you have heard from me, in the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: An outline, have thou, of healthful discourses which from me thou hast heard—with the faith and love which are in Christ Jesus:

text.NRSV: Guard the good treasure entrusted to you, with the help of the Holy Spirit living in us.
text.Rotherham: The noble thing entrusted [to thee], do thou guard, through means of the Holy Spirit which dwelleth within us.

text.NRSV: You are aware that all who are in Asia have turned away from me, including Phygelus and Hermogenes.
text.Rotherham: Thou knowest this—that all they who are in Asia have turned away from me,—of whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.

text.NRSV: May the Lord grant mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, because he often refreshed me and was not ashamed of my chain;
text.Rotherham: The Lord grant mercy unto the house, of Onesiphorus, in that, ofttimes, hath he refreshed me, and, as regardeth my chain, hath not been put to shame,

text.NRSV: when he arrived in Rome, he eagerly searched for me and found me
text.Rotherham: But, happening to be in Rome, he diligently sought out and found me,—

text.NRSV: —may the Lord grant that he will find mercy from the Lord on that day! And you know very well how much service he rendered in Ephesus.
text.Rotherham: The Lord grant him to find mercy from [the] Lord in, that, day;—and, how many times, in Ephesus, he hath ministered, better art, thou, getting to know.

text.NRSV: You then, my child, be strong in the grace that is in Christ Jesus;
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, my child, be empowering thyself in the favour that is in Christ Jesus,

text.NRSV: and what you have heard from me through many witnesses entrust to faithful people who will be able to teach others as well.
text.Rotherham: And, the things which thou hast heard from me among many witnesses, the same, entrust thou unto faithful men, such as shall be, competent, to teach, others also.

text.NRSV: Share in suffering like a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: Take thy part in suffering hardship, as a brave soldier of Christ Jesus:—

text.NRSV: No one serving in the army gets entangled in everyday affairs; the soldier’s aim is to please the enlisting officer.
text.Rotherham: No one that is serving as a soldier, entangleth himself with the matters of his livelihood, that he may please him that hath summoned him to serve as a soldier;

text.NRSV: And in the case of an athlete, no one is crowned without competing according to the rules.
text.Rotherham: If, moreover, any man, contend even in the games, he is not crowned, unless, lawfully, he contend;

text.NRSV: It is the farmer who does the work who ought to have the first share of the crops.
text.Rotherham: The toiling husbandman, ought, first, of the fruits, to partake:

text.NRSV: Think over what I say, for the Lord will give you understanding in all things.
text.Rotherham: Think, as to what I am speaking; for the Lord will give thee discernment in all things.

text.NRSV: Remember Jesus Christ, raised from the dead, a descendant of David—that is my gospel,
text.Rotherham: Keep in mind Jesus Christ—raised from among the dead, of the seed of David,—according to my joyful message:

text.NRSV: for which I suffer hardship, even to the point of being chained like a criminal. But the word of God is not chained.
text.Rotherham: In which I am suffering hardship, even unto bonds, as an evil-doer; but, the word of God, is not bound.

text.NRSV: Therefore I endure everything for the sake of the elect, so that they may also obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus, with eternal glory.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, am I enduring, all things, for the sake of the chosen, in order that, they also, may obtain, the salvation, which is in Christ Jesus along with glory age-abiding.

text.NRSV: The saying is sure: If we have died with him, we will also live with him;
text.Rotherham: Faithful, the saying—for, If we have died together, we shall also live together,

text.NRSV: if we endure, we will also reign with him; if we deny him, he will also deny us;
text.Rotherham: If we endure, we shall also reign together; If we shall deny, he also, will deny us,

text.NRSV: if we are faithless, he remains faithful— for he cannot deny himself.
text.Rotherham: If we are faithless, he, faithful, abideth,—for, deny himself, he cannot!

text.NRSV: Remind them of this, and warn them before God that they are to avoid wrangling over words, which does no good but only ruins those who are listening.
text.Rotherham: Of these things, be putting [them] in remembrance, adjuring [them] before God not to be waging word-battles,—useful, for nothing, occasioning a subversion of them that hearken.

text.NRSV: Do your best to present yourself to God as one approved by him, a worker who has no need to be ashamed, rightly explaining the word of truth.
text.Rotherham: Give diligence, thyself, approved, to present unto God,—a workman not to be put to shame, skillfully handling the word of truth.

text.NRSV: Avoid profane chatter, for it will lead people into more and more impiety,
text.Rotherham: But, the profane pratings, shun; for, unto more ungodliness, will they force themselves on;

text.NRSV: and their talk will spread like gangrene. Among them are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
text.Rotherham: And, their discourse, as a gangrene, will eat its way;—of whom are Hymenaeus and Philetus,

text.NRSV: who have swerved from the truth by claiming that the resurrection has already taken place. They are upsetting the faith of some.
text.Rotherham: Men who, concerning the truth, have erred, affirming, a resurrection, already, to have taken place, and are overthrowing the faith, of some.

text.NRSV: But God’s firm foundation stands, bearing this inscription: “The Lord knows those who are his,” and, “Let everyone who calls on the name of the Lord desist from wickedness.”
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, the firm foundation of God, standeth, having this seal—The Lord hath acknowledged them who are his, and, Let every one that nameth the name of the Lord stand aloof from unrighteousness.

text.NRSV: In a large house there are utensils not only of gold and silver but also of wood and clay, some for special use, some for ordinary.
text.Rotherham: But, in a great house, there are not only gold and silver vessels, but, also wooden and earthen: and, some, indeed, for honour, while, some, are for dishonour:

text.NRSV: All who cleanse themselves of the things I have mentioned will become special utensils, dedicated and useful to the owner of the house, ready for every good work.
text.Rotherham: If, therefore, anyone will, for pureness, sever himself from these, he shall be a vessel for honour, hallowed, meet for the Master’s use, for every good work, prepared.

text.NRSV: Shun youthful passions and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those who call on the Lord from a pure heart.
text.Rotherham: But, from the youthful covetings, flee! and pursue righteousness, faith, love, peace, along with them who call upon the Lord out of a pure heart.

text.NRSV: Have nothing to do with stupid and senseless controversies; you know that they breed quarrels.
text.Rotherham: But, from the foolish and undisciplined questionings, excuse thyself, knowing that they gender strifes;

text.NRSV: And the Lord’s servant must not be quarrelsome but kindly to everyone, an apt teacher, patient,
text.Rotherham: And, a servant of the Lord, ought not to strive, but to be, gentle, towards all, apt in teaching, ready to endure malice,—

text.NRSV: correcting opponents with gentleness. God may perhaps grant that they will repent and come to know the truth,
text.Rotherham: In meekness, bringing under discipline them that oppose themselves, lest at any time God should give them repentance unto a personal knowledge of truth,

text.NRSV: and that they may escape from the snare of the devil, having been held captive by him to do his will.
text.Rotherham: And they should wake up to sobriety out of, the adversary’s, snare,—though they have been taken alive by him for, that one’s, will.

text.NRSV: You must understand this, that in the last days distressing times will come.
text.Rotherham: But, of this, be taking note—that, in last days, there will set in perilous seasons;

text.NRSV: For people will be lovers of themselves, lovers of money, boasters, arrogant, abusive, disobedient to their parents, ungrateful, unholy,
text.Rotherham: For men will be—fond of themselves, fond of money, ostentatious, arrogant, defamers, to parents, unyielding, unthankful, unkind,

text.NRSV: inhuman, implacable, slanderers, profligates, brutes, haters of good,
text.Rotherham: without natural affection, accepting no truce, given to intrigue, without self-control, uncivilized, unfriendly to good men,

text.NRSV: treacherous, reckless, swollen with conceit, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
text.Rotherham: traitors, reckless, beclouded, lovers of pleasure more than lovers of God,

text.NRSV: holding to the outward form of godliness but denying its power. Avoid them!
text.Rotherham: having a form of godliness, but, the power thereof, denying! and, from these, turn away;

text.NRSV: For among them are those who make their way into households and captivate silly women, overwhelmed by their sins and swayed by all kinds of desires,
text.Rotherham: For, of these, are they who enter into the houses, and captivate silly women, [women] laden with sins, led on by manifold covetings,

text.NRSV: who are always being instructed and can never arrive at a knowledge of the truth.
text.Rotherham: Ever learning and never, unto a personal knowledge of truth, able to come;

text.NRSV: As Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so these people, of corrupt mind and counterfeit faith, also oppose the truth.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, like as, Jannes and Jambres, withstood Moses, so, these men also, withstand the truth,—men utterly corrupted in their mind, disapproved concerning their faith;

text.NRSV: But they will not make much progress, because, as in the case of those two men, their folly will become plain to everyone.
text.Rotherham: But they shall not force their way further, for, their folly, shall be, fully evident, unto all,—as also, that of those men, became.

text.NRSV: Now you have observed my teaching, my conduct, my aim in life, my faith, my patience, my love, my steadfastness,
text.Rotherham: But, thou, hast closely studied—my teaching, manner of life, purpose, faith, long-suffering, love, endurance,

text.NRSV: my persecutions, and my suffering the things that happened to me in Antioch, Iconium, and Lystra. What persecutions I endured! Yet the Lord rescued me from all of them.
text.Rotherham: persecutions, sufferings, what manner of things, befell me at Antioch, at Iconium, at Lystra, what manner of persecutions, I endured,—and, out of all, the Lord, rescued me;—

text.NRSV: Indeed, all who want to live a godly life in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
text.Rotherham: Yea and, all who are determined to live in a godly manner in Christ Jesus, will be persecuted;

text.NRSV: But wicked people and impostors will go from bad to worse, deceiving others and being deceived.
text.Rotherham: Whereas, wicked men and howling impostors, will force their way to the worse, deceiving and being deceived.

text.NRSV: But as for you, continue in what you have learned and firmly believed, knowing from whom you learned it,
text.Rotherham: But, thou,—abide in the things which thou hast learned, and been entrusted with, knowing, from whom, thou hast learned [them],—

text.NRSV: and how from childhood you have known the sacred writings that are able to instruct you for salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And that, from a babe, those sacred letters, thou hast known which are able to make thee wise unto salvation, through the faith which is in Christ Jesus:

text.NRSV: All scripture is inspired by God and is useful for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness,
text.Rotherham: Every scripture, [is] God-breathed, and profitable—unto teaching, unto conviction, unto correction, unto the discipline that is in righteousness,—

text.NRSV: so that everyone who belongs to God may be proficient, equipped for every good work.
text.Rotherham: In order that, ready, may be the man, of God, unto every good work, being well-prepared.

text.NRSV: In the presence of God and of Christ Jesus, who is to judge the living and the dead, and in view of his appearing and his kingdom, I solemnly urge you:
text.Rotherham: I adjure [thee] before God, and Christ Jesus—Who is about to be judging living and dead,—both as to his forthshining and his kingdom,

text.NRSV: proclaim the message; be persistent whether the time is favorable or unfavorable; convince, rebuke, and encourage, with the utmost patience in teaching.
text.Rotherham: Proclaim the word, take thy position—in season, out of season,—convince, rebuke, encourage,—with all long-suffering and teaching.

text.NRSV: For the time is coming when people will not put up with sound doctrine, but having itching ears, they will accumulate for themselves teachers to suit their own desires,
text.Rotherham: For there will be a season—when, the healthful teaching, they will not endure, but, according to their own covetings, will, unto themselves, heap up teachers, because they have an itching ear,

text.NRSV: and will turn away from listening to the truth and wander away into myths.
text.Rotherham: And, from the truth, indeed, their ear, will they turn away, while, unto stories, they will turn themselves aside.

text.NRSV: As for you, always be sober, endure suffering, do the work of an evangelist, carry out your ministry fully.
text.Rotherham: But, thou,—be sober in all things, suffer hardship, do, the work, of an evangelist, thy ministry, completely fulfill;

text.NRSV: As for me, I am already being poured out as a libation, and the time of my departure has come.
text.Rotherham: For, I, already, am being poured out as a drink-offering, and, the season of my release, is at hand,—

text.NRSV: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the race, I have kept the faith.
text.Rotherham: The noble contest, have I contested, the race, have I finished, the faith, have I kept:

text.NRSV: From now on there is reserved for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give me on that day, and not only to me but also to all who have longed for his appearing.
text.Rotherham: Henceforth, lieth by for me—the crown, of righteousness, which the Lord will render unto me in that, day,—The righteous judge,—Ye, not alone unto me, but unto all them also who have loved his forthshining.

text.NRSV: Do your best to come to me soon,
text.Rotherham: Give diligence to come unto me speedily,

text.NRSV: for Demas, in love with this present world, has deserted me and gone to Thessalonica; Crescens has gone to Galatia, Titus to Dalmatia.
text.Rotherham: For, Demas, hath forsaken me, having loved the present age, and hath journeyed unto Thessalonica; Crescens unto Galatia, Titus unto Dalmatia:

text.NRSV: Only Luke is with me. Get Mark and bring him with you, for he is useful in my ministry.
text.Rotherham: Luke, alone is with me. Receiving, Mark, back, bring him with thyself, for he is very useful to me for ministering;

text.NRSV: I have sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
text.Rotherham: and, Tychicus, have I sent unto Ephesus.

text.NRSV: When you come, bring the cloak that I left with Carpus at Troas, also the books, and above all the parchments.
text.Rotherham: The cloak that I left in Troas, with Carpus, when thou comest, bring; and the scrolls, especially, the parchments.

text.NRSV: Alexander the coppersmith did me great harm; the Lord will pay him back for his deeds.
text.Rotherham: Alexander the coppersmith, of much baseness towards me, hath given proof,—the Lord will render unto him according to his works.—

text.NRSV: You also must beware of him, for he strongly opposed our message.
text.Rotherham: Of whom, be, thou also, on thy guard, for he hath greatly withstood our words.

text.NRSV: At my first defense no one came to my support, but all deserted me. May it not be counted against them!
text.Rotherham: In my first defence, no man, came in to help me, but, all, forsook me,—unto them, may it not be reckoned!—

text.NRSV: But the Lord stood by me and gave me strength, so that through me the message might be fully proclaimed and all the Gentiles might hear it. So I was rescued from the lion’s mouth.
text.Rotherham: But, the Lord, stood by me, and empowered me, in order that, through me, the proclamation, might be fully made, and, all the nations, might hear; and I was delivered out of the mouth of a lion:—

text.NRSV: The Lord will rescue me from every evil attack and save me for his heavenly kingdom. To him be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: The Lord will rescue me from every wicked work, and will bring me safe into his heavenly kingdom: unto whom be the glory, unto the ages of ages. Amen.

text.NRSV: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.
text.Rotherham: Salute Prisca and Aquila and the house of Onesiphorus.

text.NRSV: Erastus remained in Corinth; Trophimus I left ill in Miletus.
text.Rotherham: Erastus, remained in Corinth; but, Trophimus, I left at Miletus, sick.

text.NRSV: Do your best to come before winter. Eubulus sends greetings to you, as do Pudens and Linus and Claudia and all the brothers and sisters.
text.Rotherham: Give diligence to come, before winter. There salute thee—Eubulus, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and [all] the brethren.

text.NRSV: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you.
text.Rotherham: The Lord, be with thy spirit. Favour, be with you.
